response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts. One way to identify these accounts is by looking for inconsistencies in their profiles, such as mismatched names or images. Additionally, paying attention to the content they post can help you distinguish between real and fake accounts. Fake accounts often post sensational or misleading content to gain attention, while real accounts tend to post more informative and accurate content. Another way to manage fake content is by using fact-checking websites or social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools. These tools can help you verify the accuracy of the information being shared on social media. Furthermore, it is important to be cautious when interacting with unknown accounts and to avoid engaging with them until they have proven themselves to be trustworthy. By taking these steps, individuals can protect themselves from falling victim to fake content on social media.",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts, such as inconsistencies in their profiles or sensational/misleading content. Fact-checking websites and social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools can also help verify the accuracy of information being shared. Additionally, individuals should be cautious when interacting with unknown accounts and avoid engaging with them until they have proven themselves to be trustworthy.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts. One way to identify these accounts is by looking for inconsistencies in their profiles, such as mismatched names or images. Additionally, paying attention to the content they post can help you distinguish between real and fake accounts. Fake accounts often post sensational or misleading content to gain attention, while real accounts tend to post more informative and accurate content. Another way to manage fake content is by using fact-checking websites or social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools. These tools can help you verify the accuracy of the information being shared on social media. Furthermore, it is important to be cautious when interacting with unknown accounts and to avoid engaging with them until they have proven themselves to be trustworthy. By taking these steps, individuals can protect themselves from falling victim to fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts, such as inconsistencies in their profiles or sensational/misleading content. Fact-checking websites and social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools can also help verify the accuracy of information being shared. Additionally, individuals should be cautious when interacting with unknown accounts and avoid engaging with them until they have proven themselves to be trustworthy.
